steps.title: Turn the rate into table rows
steps.content: Juan rides at $5.7$ minutes per mile. That means $0$ miles takes $0$ minutes, $1$ mile takes $5.7$ minutes, and $2$ miles takes $11.4$ minutes.

steps.title: Find the line from the data
steps.content: The relationship is proportional because time equals rate times distance: $$\text{time}=5.7\cdot \text{distance}.$$ So the function is $$m(x)=5.7x.$$ The coefficient $5.7$ is the number of minutes per mile.

steps.title: Choose the matching function
steps.content: Choice D, $m(x)=5.7x$, matches the rate and the units: minutes per mile times miles equals minutes. Choices A, B, and C use division, addition, or subtraction instead of multiplying by the rate. Choice D is correct.
